title: East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: The East Karbi Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Assam state of India.
overview.description: The East Karbi Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Assam state of India. It is situated 35 km from the city of Diphu, Karbi Anglong district. It covers an area of 221.81 km2, and its elevation varies between 80 and 500 meter above sea level. The forest area was declared a wildlife sanctuary on 27 July 2000 by the Assam State Government.

topPlaces.name: Karbi Youth Festival
topPlaces.description: Karbi Youth Festival is an annual festival celebrated by the Karbi People living in East and West Karbi Anglong district and other districts in the Northeast Indian state of Assam, with occasional participation from other tribes and states of Northeastern India. It is considered the oldest ethnic festival in India.

topPlaces.name: Manja, India
topPlaces.description: Manja is a small town in the Karbi Anglong district of Assam in India. It lies on the banks of the Chomna (Yamuna) river and is surrounded by scenic blue hills.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Diphu
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Diphu (Latin: Diphuen(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Diphu in the ecclesiastical province of Guwahati in India.

topPlaces.name: 2013 Jhankar Saikia mob lynching
topPlaces.description: 2013 Jhankar Saikia Mob Lynching happened in Diphu town, Karbi Anglong district, Assam, in India on 25 June 2013 when Diphu College student Jhankar Saikia and his father, Haren Saikia, refused to pay an auto-rickshaw fare of Rs. 30, when the regular fare was Rs.

items.note: Karbi Youth Festival is an annual festival celebrated by the Karbi People living in East and West Karbi Anglong district and other districts in the Northeast Indian state of Assam, with occasional participation from other tribes and states of Northeastern India. It is considered the oldest ethnic festival in India.
Manja is a small town in the Karbi Anglong district of Assam in India. It lies on the banks of the Chomna (Yamuna) river and is surrounded by scenic blue hills.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Diphu (Latin: Diphuen(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Diphu in the ecclesiastical province of Guwahati in India.

2013 Jhankar Saikia Mob Lynching happened in Diphu town, Karbi Anglong district, Assam, in India on 25 June 2013 when Diphu College student Jhankar Saikia and his father, Haren Saikia, refused to pay an auto-rickshaw fare of Rs. 30, when the regular fare was Rs.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in East Karbi-Anglong Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karbi Youth Festival, Manja, India, Diocese of Diphu, 2013 Jhankar Saikia mob lynching.
